claim: 95% of users don't need agent.md or claude.md files because models are already good; adding them wastes tokens and degrades performance as context fills
actionable: Remove agent.md files unless they contain proprietary company knowledge; rely on skills with progressive disclosure instead
source_title: How AI agents & Claude skills work (Clearly Explained)

claim: Skills use progressive disclosure: only the title and description sit in context until the agent realizes it needs the full skill, saving thousands of tokens per conversation
actionable: Build skills with concise descriptions instead of putting all instructions in agent.md; agents will load full skill content only when needed
source_title: How AI agents & Claude skills work (Clearly Explained)

claim: Teaching agents through step-by-step workflow walkthroughs before converting to skills yields 100% success rate vs downloading pre-built skills that fail
actionable: Walk through your exact workflow with the agent conversationally, iterate until it succeeds, then ask it to review and create the skill
source_title: How AI agents & Claude skills work (Clearly Explained)

claim: Models are token predictors, not thinkers; they map English to vector space and find closest resemblance, so they need explicit step-by-step guidance
actionable: Don't expect agents to infer multi-step workflows from high-level requests; explicitly teach each step in the workflow before automating
source_title: How AI agents & Claude skills work (Clearly Explained)

claim: Recursively building skills by iterating with agents when they fail, fixing the failure, and updating the skill yields highly effective automation over 5+ iterations
actionable: When an agent fails a task, ask why, pass the error back, let it fix itself, then ask it to update the skill to prevent recurrence
source_title: How AI agents & Claude skills work (Clearly Explained)

claim: Don't download pre-made skills from marketplaces; build your own customized to your workflow because agents need context of successful runs in your domain
actionable: Avoid using off-the-shelf skills; invest time building domain-specific skills tailored to your exact workflows and success criteria
source_title: How AI agents & Claude skills work (Clearly Explained)

claim: Start with one agent and scale up to sub-agents only after you have predefined, working workflows; don't create sub-agents for visual appeal
actionable: Build single-agent workflows first with proven results, then add sub-agents when you have clear domain separation and redundant tasks
source_title: How AI agents & Claude skills work (Clearly Explained)

claim: Context window optimization: keep usage between 10-70% of max capacity; above 80% models degrade in quality just like humans overloaded with information
actionable: Monitor context window fill rate; compact conversations when approaching 70% to maintain quality and avoid token waste near limits
source_title: How AI agents & Claude skills work (Clearly Explained)

claim: Don't include generic tech stack info (React, Next.js) in agent.md files; the code itself has become context, so solid templates matter more than instruction
actionable: Invest in quality project templates instead of verbose agent instructions; agents can infer tech stack from the codebase
source_title: How AI agents & Claude skills work (Clearly Explained)
